game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Five Nights at Freddy's:** Players assume the role of a night security guard, confined to an office, tasked with surviving from 12 AM to 6 AM for five consecutive nights against hostile animatronic robots. The primary interaction involves tapping on a monitor to cycle through security cameras, tracking the animatronics' movements across the pizza parlor, while a limited power supply depletes with every action. Players must strategically use power by tapping buttons to activate hallway lights to check for immediate threats or close two security doors, each consuming a specific amount of power per second. If an animatronic reaches the office and the doors are open, the game ends in a jumpscare, and difficulty scales each night with increased animatronic aggression and faster power depletion rates.
game_info.description: You are a night security guard at Freddy's Pizza Parlor. Survive from 12 AM to 6 AM for five consecutive nights while hostile animatronics hunt you. Monitor security cameras to track their movements, but be careful—every action drains your limited power supply. If power runs out or an animatronic reaches your office with the doors open, it's game over. Each night gets progressively harder with more aggressive enemies and faster power drain.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate camera feeds and UI elements
Space: Toggle camera monitor view
Z: Toggle left door (hold to keep closed)
Shift: Toggle right door (hold to keep closed)
Left Arrow (when not in camera): Check left hallway light
Right Arrow (when not in camera): Check right hallway light
Enter: Start game (from start screen)
Esc: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Basic functionality test. Validates that the player can interact with core mechanics: opening camera monitor, switching between cameras, closing doors, and checking lights. Tests power consumption and basic animatronic movement.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Rapidly cycle through all available actions including camera switching, door toggling, and light checking. Monitor power consumption to ensure it depletes appropriately. Track animatronic positions to validate movement system.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Test successfully validates all controls work correctly, power depletes with usage, animatronics move through camera locations, and game responds appropriately to player actions without errors.

TEST_1.expected: Test successfully validates all controls work correctly, power depletes with usage, animatronics move through camera locations, and game responds appropriately to player actions without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Win condition test. Implements an optimal survival strategy to successfully complete Night 1 by reaching 6 AM with power remaining. Tests the balance between monitoring threats and conserving power.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Strategically check cameras to locate animatronics. Only close doors when animatronics are nearby (detected via cameras or lights). Minimize power usage by keeping doors open when safe. Use lights to verify immediate threats before closing doors.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Player successfully survives until 6 AM on Night 1, triggering the GAME_OVER_WIN state. Power management is efficient enough to last the entire night while keeping animatronics at bay.

TEST_2.expected: Player successfully survives until 6 AM on Night 1, triggering the GAME_OVER_WIN state. Power management is efficient enough to last the entire night while keeping animatronics at bay.

TEST_3.test_description: Door defense test. Validates that closed doors successfully prevent animatronics from entering the office and triggering a jumpscare. Tests the core survival mechanic.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Keep both doors closed for extended periods when animatronics are detected nearby. Monitor power drain from continuous door usage. Verify that animatronics cannot enter while doors are secured.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Animatronics detected at doors are successfully blocked when doors are closed. Power drains quickly from continuous door usage, but no jumpscares occur while doors remain secured.

TEST_4.test_description: Power depletion test. Validates lose condition when power reaches zero. Tests that all systems shut down and animatronics can freely enter the office.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Intentionally waste power by keeping both doors closed continuously and constantly checking cameras and lights. Monitor power percentage until it reaches zero.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Power depletes to zero, triggering emergency mode where all systems fail. An animatronic enters shortly after, resulting in GAME_OVER_LOSE state.
TEST_4.description: Power depletion test. Validates lose condition when power reaches zero. Tests that all systems shut down and animatronics can freely enter the office.
TEST_4.strategy: Intentionally waste power by keeping both doors closed continuously and constantly checking cameras and lights. Monitor power percentage until it reaches zero.
TEST_4.expected: Power depletes to zero, triggering emergency mode where all systems fail. An animatronic enters shortly after, resulting in GAME_OVER_LOSE state.

TEST_5.test_description: Multi-night progression test. Validates that difficulty increases across nights with more aggressive animatronics and faster power drain. Tests game progression system.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Use winning strategy to progress through multiple nights. Monitor animatronic behavior patterns and power consumption rates across different nights to detect difficulty scaling.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Each subsequent night shows measurably increased difficulty through faster animatronic movement and higher power drain rates, requiring more precise resource management to survive.
